titleStmt.title: Letter to Sir John Robert O'Connell, 28 March 1916
Letters 1916-1923

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: This letter was addressed to Sir John Robert O'Connell (1868-1943) a solicitor who made an application for the vacant position of chief crown solicitor for Ireland following the death of Malachy Kelly (1851-1916) who had previously held the position. The letter acknowledges O'Connell's application and informs him that it will be considered before a decision is made.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary's Office Registered Papers; CSO RP/1916/7153/9

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
28th March, 1916. Dear Sir John, Thank you for your letter of yesterday's
date applying for the position of Chief Crown Solicitor.
I will see that the application is duly recorded and
that your eminent public services are not lost sight of
when it is considered. Yours sincerely,
Sir John Robert O'Connell, 34, Kildare Street,
DUBLIN.
